sources.notes: Statement and constraints: edit the string to choose a count of non-overlapping occurrences of 'docker' that maximizes invited friends, then minimize edits.
Official editorial/code confirms interval-count sweep for the best occurrence count and Lagrangian DP with binary search for creating occurrences.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate checked by search/open attempt; not used as a consulted solution source.

annotation.summary: Choose how many times the word 'docker' appears after editing the string to maximize satisfied interval requests, and among such choices minimize changed characters.
annotation.constraints: n and number of requests are large enough that exact-count string DP for every k is too slow; occurrences of 'docker' are length 6 and cannot overlap with another valid occurrence.
annotation.core_idea: First count existing occurrences and use a difference array over requested [l,r] occurrence counts to find the best target k. For k <= current occurrences, delete extra occurrences with one change each. For k > current occurrences, selecting occurrences is a weighted non-overlapping interval DP over length-6 blocks. Add a penalty per chosen block, binary-search the penalty (Aliens trick), and run a linear DP that either skips a position or places 'docker' there with mismatch cost plus penalty; this recovers the minimum edit cost for the smallest optimal target k above the current count.
annotation.complexity: O((n + q) + n log n log C) style, depending on penalty bounds
tricks: choose target occurrence count by request coverage
Lagrangian penalty turns exact count into unconstrained DP
annotation.main_agent_review_notes: The DP and penalty search match the official implementation, but the full optimality proof for choosing the smallest optimal k above the current count should be rechecked by the main agent.

solution_variants.name: aliens-nonoverlap-word-dp
solution_variants.summary: Binary-search a per-occurrence penalty and run linear DP over positions, with transitions skip or place a non-overlapping 'docker' block.

solution_variants.name: delete-existing-occurrences
solution_variants.summary: If the optimal target count is not above the current number of occurrences, change one character in each surplus existing occurrence.

tags.evidence: The hard case is solved by a position DP selecting non-overlapping length-6 blocks.
A binary-searched penalty controls the number of selected occurrences in the DP.
The penalty parameter is binary-searched to reach the required occurrence count.
The word has no border, so valid occurrences can be treated as disjoint length-6 intervals in the DP.
tags.description: Use a structural reason that chosen patterns or intervals cannot overlap, reducing a string/interval problem to disjoint selection.
tags.created_reason: The no-self-overlap property is a reusable string/interval selection reduction not captured by plain dp.
